Create a single tmux session and re-exec self inside it with `--count 1`.\nfn launch_in_tmux(\n    session_name: &str,\n    workspace: Option<PathBuf>,\n    agent: Option<String>,\n    identifier: Option<String>,\n    timeout: Option<u64>,\n) -> Result<()> {\n    // Build the command that tmux will run inside the session.\n    let self_exe = std::env::current_exe().context(\"failed to locate own binary\")?;\n    let mut inner_args = vec![\"run\".to_string()];\n\n    if let Some(ref ws) = workspace {\n        inner_args.push(\"--workspace\".to_string());\n inner_args.push(t.to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Build stderr log path: ~/.needle/logs/<session_name>.stderr.log\n    let home = std::env::var(\"HOME\").unwrap_or_else(|_| \".\".to_string());\n    let remaining.len();\n\n    log::info!(\n        \"[worker] scale-down complete: {} graceful, {} force-killed\",\n        result.graceful,\n        result.force_killed\n    );\n\n    result\n}\n\n/// Find workers to stop, preferring idle workers.\n///\n/// Returns up to `n` session names, sorted by idle status and heartbeat age.\nfn find_workers_to_stop(n: usize, config: &WorkerConfig) -> Vec<String> {\n    let heartbeats = read_heartbeats(&config.heartbeat_dir, &config.session_prefix);\n\n    // Sort workers: idle first, then by heartbeat age (oldest first)\n    let mut workers: Vec<_> = heartbeats.into_iter().collect();\n    workers.sort_by(|a, b| {\n        // Prefer idle workers","numLines":120,"startLine":260,"totalLines":693}}}

The governor sends `C-c` via `tmux send-keys`. This sends the character to the bash shell, which interprets it as SIGINT and exits. But the needle worker running as a child of bash doesn't receive SIGINT - bash just dies, and the needle worker becomes orphaned and gets reaped (or SIGHUP when its parent dies).\n\nThe issue is that:\n1. Tmux session command: `NEEDLE_INNER=1 needle run ... 2>> stderr.log; tmux kill-session -t session`\n2. Bash runs needle as a child process\n3. Governor sends `C-c` to the bash shell\n4. Bash exits (interpreting C-c as SIGINT)\n5. Needle worker loses parent and receives SIGHUP (or gets reaped without signaling)\n6. The sleep completes after 900 seconds, but by then something else has happened\n\nWait, looking more carefully at the handle_exhausted code - the idle sleep is cancellable! It checks the shutdown flag every 1 second. So if SIGHUP sets the flag, the worker should wake up within 1 second and call stop().\n\nLet me re-examine the evidence. The issue says:\n- Worker stays alive for exactly 900 seconds (the idle timeout)\n- No worker.stopped event\n- No stderr output\n\nThis suggests:\n1. The SIGHUP handler is NOT being triggered (or not working)\n\n\n2. Or the worker dies after the sleep completes but before transitioning to SELECTING\n\nI'm investigating a subtle signal handling and process interaction problem. The core challenge seems to be how signals are propagated between bash, tmux, and the needle worker process.
